AssistantIntent.description: Coarse intent classification attached to assistant turns. Used by the UI to choose the appropriate rendering style (e.g. `SummaryConfirm` → show the confirm card; `Blocker` → show the blocker card).

oneOf.description: Opening turn of a new session.
Acknowledgement of SME input with no structural change.
Asking the SME to clarify an ambiguous aspect of the intake.
Presenting the plan summary card for SME confirmation.
Sharing an intermediate recommendation or question.
Follow-up turn after a successful emission.
Surfacing a blocker that requires SME action.

ConfirmationCard.description: Plan-summary card rendered by `propose_summary_confirmation`. Persisted on the turn so the UI can re-render after page reload.

resource_estimate.description: Coarse resource estimate aggregated from each atom's `resource_profile`. Rendered inline so the SME sees a "≈ N core-hours, peak M GB" cost preview before clicking Accept. None for legacy / non-composer paths.

summary_hash.description:
SHA-256 hex digest of `summary_markdown` at the moment the card was raised. The UI renders the leading 12 hex chars as a "summary fingerprint" so the SME can verify the durable audit record references the exact text they saw. The `/confirm` endpoint attaches the same hash to the `DecisionType::Confirm` audit record, so a later replayer can match the recorded fingerprint to the text in `intake-conversation.jsonl` and detect tampering / drift between the displayed card and the confirmed text.

`#[serde(default)]` so existing on-disk sessions whose cards pre-date this field deserialize cleanly with an empty hash — the legacy records carry no fingerprint, which is fine because they also pre-date the matching audit-record field.

summary_markdown.description: Markdown summary of the current plan presented to the SME for approval.

ResourceEstimate.description:
Aggregated resource estimate for a composition. Coarse numeric form so the SME sees ballpark numbers instead of opaque "moderate/large" buckets. Pilot sizing replaces these projections with measured numbers at execution time; this is the pre-approval preview.

Bucket → number table (intentionally conservative; tuned to over-estimate slightly so the SME isn't surprised): - `cpu`: light → 2, moderate → 8, heavy → 32, very_heavy → 64 cores - `memory`: small → 4, medium → 16, large → 64, xl → 256 GB - `runtime_class`: seconds → 0.01, minutes → 0.5, hours → 4 hr

estimated_cost_usd.description: Optional dollar projection — left `None` here (pilot's `estimate_cost_usd` populates it when SWFC_PILOT_ENABLED=1 surfaces the per-atom cost rate).

gpu_task_count.description: Count of atoms whose `resource_profile.gpu` is true.

peak_memory_gb.description: Largest single-atom `memory` bucket midpoint, in GB. Bounds the worst-case instance footprint.

total_core_hours.description: Sum across atoms of `cpu_cores × runtime_hours`. Coarse rollup; the SME-facing card renders this as "≈ N core-hours".

total_memory_gb.description: Sum of per-atom `memory` bucket midpoints, in GB.

ToolCallRecord.description: Audit record for a single tool call made during a turn.

args.description: Arguments the LLM passed to the tool.

is_error.description: True when the dispatcher returned a `ToolError`.

model.description: Anthropic model id that produced this tool call.

timestamp.description: UTC timestamp when the tool was dispatched.

tool_name.description: Canonical snake_case name of the tool (matches `Tool::name()`).

turn_id.description: Turn this tool call belongs to.

TurnRole.description: Authorship role of a conversation turn.

oneOf.description: Message authored by the LLM on behalf of the assistant.
Injected system message (not shown in the SME-facing chat pane).
One conversation turn (user, assistant, or system message).

confirmation_card.description: Confirmation card rendered for this turn, if the assistant called `propose_summary_confirmation`.

intent.description: Coarse intent classification for assistant turns (used by the UI to pick a rendering style).

quick_replies.description: Quick-reply chips the assistant suggested at the end of this turn.

timestamp.description: UTC timestamp when this turn was created.

tool_calls.description: Tool calls the assistant made during this turn.

turn_id.description: Stable identifier for this turn (UUID v4).

args.description: Arguments the LLM passed to the tool.

is_error.description: True when the dispatcher returned a `ToolError`.

model.description: Anthropic model id that produced this tool call.

timestamp.description: UTC timestamp when the tool was dispatched.

tool_name.description: Canonical snake_case name of the tool (matches `Tool::name()`).

turn_id.description: Turn this tool call belongs to.
